title: Cron: Log Rotation Is Silently Failing
description:
## The Situation

Your log rotation job has been 'running' every 5 minutes for weeks. Except `/var/log/app/app.log` is now hundreds of megabytes — it has never actually been rotated.

The cron job shows up in `crontab -l`. The rotation script works when you call it directly. But the archives directory is empty.

## Your Task

1. Read the crontab and identify what's wrong
2. Understand *why* it fails silently (no error emails, no logs)
3. Fix the crontab entry
4. Verify the script runs correctly

## Useful Commands

```bash
# See the scheduled job
crontab -l

# Check when logs were last rotated
cat /var/log/rotate-status.log
ls -lh /var/log/app/archive/

# Try running the script directly
rotate-logs.sh               # try it this way first
/usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh   # then try with full path

# See where executables live
which rotate-logs.sh 2>/dev/null || echo 'not in PATH'
ls -la /usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh

# Edit the crontab
crontab -e
```
hints: The script works when you call it directly but not from cron. The difference is the environment — specifically, what PATH is available.
Cron uses a minimal PATH: /usr/bin:/bin. Your script lives in /usr/local/bin. Cron simply can't find it by its short name.
Fix the crontab entry to use the full path: replace 'rotate-logs.sh' with '/usr/local/bin/rotate-logs.sh'. Or add PATH=/usr/local/bin:/usr/bin:/bin as the first line of your crontab.
